name: Tindle
concept: A shy, hand-thumbed terracotta imp whose whole head is an unstruck match tip and whose left forearm is a gritty striker band — he could light himself at any moment, and he has never dared, so he hugs that arm across his warm little belly.
imagePrompt:
A chunky handmade stop-motion plasticine/clay creature, full body, standing, facing the viewer, sculpted in warm terracotta.

SILHOUETTE: one single connected blob-figure, unmistakable and closed. Squat bowling-pin body — a heavy pear-shaped belly that sits low and wide, tapering into a short thick neck. Two stubby armless-looking mitt arms with no fingers, rounded like sausage nubs. Two big flat pad feet splayed slightly outward, wide enough to plant him solidly. Head is a smooth rounded dome, oversized (about 45% of total height), sitting directly on the shoulders with almost no neck. Overall outline reads as a soft cartoon egg on two boots — clean, bold, no gaps, no thin parts, nothing detached, nothing wispy.

THE ONE IDEA — HE IS AN UNSTRUCK MATCH: the top of his rounded head is capped in a thick matte deep-vermilion sulphur crown, a fat rounded dollop of dull match-tip red that covers the whole upper skull like a sculpted beanie, with a soft crumbly clay edge where it meets the terracotta. And his left forearm is a wide band of gritty dark charcoal striker-strip clay — coarse sandpaper texture pressed into the arm, a solid thick cuff, no fringe. He hugs that striker arm shyly across his belly, holding it in with his other mitt, keeping himself unlit. That is the whole character: a match that is scared to strike itself, being gentle with its own dangerous arm.

PALETTE: one bold hero colour — warm terracotta orange-clay body (sun-baked flowerpot, slightly rosy). Deep matte vermilion head cap. Charcoal-grey grit band on the forearm. Soft cream-ivory belly plate, a rounded oval patch of paler clay pressed onto the front like a stone-ware apron, with a faint warm amber glow deep inside it — a low, contained ember light blooming from within the clay itself, softly diffused, never leaving the body. Dusty rose blush on both cheeks.

FACE: big honest eyes — two large glossy dark-chocolate bead eyes, wide and round, set low and far apart, each with one clean white highlight and one tiny lower catchlight. Simple soft brow ridges pressed above them for a slightly worried, hopeful tilt. No visible nose, just a gentle sculpted bump. A small, closed, thoughtful mouth — a short upturned curve pressed into the clay, shy and kind. He is looking straight at you as if asking whether it is okay to exist.

MATERIAL — CLAY, COMMITTED: visibly hand-sculpted. Thumbprints and fingerprint whorls pressed all over the body, a soft kneaded seam running down one side, tiny tool marks around the eyes, slightly uneven rolled-clay limbs. Matte plasticine surface with a faint waxy sheen where fingers smoothed it, tiny bits of grit in the clay. Soft warm studio key light from the upper left, gentle bounce fill, a soft short contact shadow directly beneath the feet. Chunky, weighty, squeezable, made by hands.

Rendered clean and crisp with thick readable forms; every element fused to the body; no particles, no sparks, no flame, no smoke, no strands, no text.
